title: Best VPN for Linux in 2026: Command Line and GUI Options Compared
description: Most VPNs work best on Windows. Here are the Linux VPNs that actually work well, including native clients and command-line options.
content:
Linux users often get overlooked by VPN providers. Most VPNs offer polished apps for Windows and macOS while the Linux version is either a basic command-line client or nothing at all. A few providers take Linux seriously, and those are the ones worth recommending.

## What to Look for in a Linux VPN

A good Linux VPN should offer a native GUI app or at minimum a well-maintained CLI client with clear documentation. OpenVPN and WireGuard support are standard; WireGuard is preferable for performance on modern kernels. Killswitch support on Linux is less reliable than on Windows, so check whether it works with your specific distribution before committing.

## Mullvad VPN

Mullvad has one of the best Linux experiences of any VPN provider. It offers a native GUI app that works on Ubuntu, Fedora, and Debian-based distributions, and a fully functional CLI tool for headless servers. WireGuard is the default protocol. Mullvad does not require an account email, only a randomly generated account number. This makes it the most privacy-forward option on this list. Price is 5 euros per month flat.

## ProtonVPN

ProtonVPN offers a native Linux GUI and CLI. The app supports multiple protocols including WireGuard and OpenVPN, and the killswitch works reliably on most mainstream distributions. ProtonVPN has a solid free tier with unlimited bandwidth (but limited to specific servers), which is worth considering if you want to test it before paying.

## NordVPN

NordVPN offers a Linux CLI client that is well-maintained with regular updates. The GUI is not native; you control it entirely from the command line. The documentation is clear and setup is straightforward. NordVPN is a good choice if you are already a customer on other platforms and want to extend to Linux without paying for a new subscription.

## ExpressVPN

ExpressVPN has a Linux CLI client that works reliably. It supports its Lightway protocol on Linux in addition to OpenVPN. The setup process requires more manual steps than Mullvad or Proton, but ExpressVPN's support documentation for Linux is detailed. A good pick if you need consistent performance across many server locations.

## WireGuard Manual Setup

For advanced users, any VPN provider that supports WireGuard can be used with Linux's built-in WireGuard kernel module without a dedicated app. Providers like IVPN and AzireVPN offer clean WireGuard config file generators. This approach gives you full control and avoids closed-source client software, at the cost of slightly more setup time.
